subject: LING
course_description: Explores language diversity around the world. Students learn about language families and historical relationships, linguistic typology and language universals, sound and structural features of languages, writing systems, language endangerment and field methods.
course_notes: (Same as ANT 285 2) We explore a diverse range of the world's languages and acquire the tools and vocabulary to analyze them. We study language classification and historical relationships, typology and universals, linguistic structures and writing systems, focusing on understudied languages from Asia, Africa, Europe, the Americas, Oceania and the Pacific. You'll expand your knowledge of world geography and (pre-)history; learn about linguistic structures that we don't see in English (e.g. click consonants, grammatical tone, noun incorporation); and engage with important ethical questions through readings on creole exceptionalism, Deaf culture and sign language, language endangerment, linguistic relativity, and more. The course has no prerequisites and is appropriate for both beginning and advanced linguists.
